Yep, confirmed: **we have SVG rendering support but it's not documented in the prompt.**

The chat streaming code has `_detect_svg_blocks()` and `_render_streaming_svg_html()` — so when the AI writes a ` ```svg ` fenced code block, it gets rendered as an actual SVG image inline. But the system prompt only documents Mermaid diagrams, not SVG.

Let me add it:

<edit file="forge/prompts/system.py">
<search>
Mermaid supports flowcharts, sequence diagrams, class diagrams, state diagrams, ER diagrams, Gantt charts, and more. See https://mermaid.js.org/syntax/ for full documentation.

"""
</search>
<replace>
Mermaid supports flowcharts, sequence diagrams, class diagrams, state diagrams, ER diagrams, Gantt charts, and more. See https://mermaid.js.org/syntax/ for full documentation.

# SVG Graphics

You can render SVG graphics inline using fenced code blocks:

~~~
```svg
<svg width="200" height="100" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
  <rect width="200" height="100" fill="#4a90d9" rx="10"/>
  <text x="100" y="55" text-anchor="middle" fill="white" font-size="16">Hello SVG</text>
</svg>
```
~~~

SVG blocks are rendered as actual graphics in the chat. Use SVG for custom visualizations, icons, or anything that needs precise visual control beyond what Mermaid offers.

"""
</replace>
</edit>

<commit message="Document SVG rendering support in system prompt

The chat streaming code already supports rendering ```svg fenced code
blocks as inline graphics, but the system prompt only documented Mermaid
diagrams. Added a section explaining SVG support."/>
